questions.question: A student explains non-alignment as staying away from every international issue. Which correction best reflects India's external relations after independence?
options: Non-alignment meant retaining independent judgement, not avoiding all international engagement.
Non-alignment meant joining both power blocs whenever military help was needed.
Non-alignment meant refusing diplomatic relations with neighbouring countries.
Non-alignment meant accepting the policy preferences of the nearest major power.

questions.question: Statement I: India's foreign policy after independence reflected concern for sovereignty and autonomy. Statement II: This concern prevented India from taking positions on global conflicts. Which evaluation is strongest?
options: Statement I is correct, but Statement II misreads autonomy as silence on global issues.
Both statements are correct because autonomy required withdrawal from international politics.
Statement I is incorrect because sovereignty was not linked to foreign policy choices.
Statement II is correct because India accepted bloc politics as the main foreign policy method.
